theme: The future of corporate events — what AI can't replace

linkedin.text:
AI can plan your event. It can't save it at 11pm when the bus breaks down.

The tools are genuinely impressive — automated scheduling, personalized agendas, smart breakout allocation. But after 200+ events and 16 years, here's what AI still can't do:

The vendor who drives 4 hours at 11pm because a coach died on the highway — AI didn't hire him. I did, 8 years ago.

The Prague fixer who knows which owner opens his private room for 50 people on 2 hours' notice — AI can't text him. I can.

The moment a client CEO realizes the event isn't a budget line anymore — it's the highlight of their people's year — doesn't come from a dashboard.

AI will make our industry faster and smarter. But clients come back a 5th and 8th time for one reason: trust that when something breaks — and it always does — the right person is already on the phone.

That's still a human business.

💬 DM us "HUMAN" if you'd rather have a person on the other end of your event.

#EventProduction #IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #EmployeeEngagement #B2BEvents #MICE #Leadership

instagram.text:
AI can plan your event. It can't save it at 11pm. 🤖

What it still can't do:
→ Drive 4 hours at midnight when the bus dies
→ Text the Prague owner who opens his private room on 2h notice
→ Make a CEO realize the event became the highlight of their team's year

AI makes us faster. Trust is still human.

facebook.text:
Everyone's asking if AI will replace event planners. Let me tell you about a phone call at 11pm. 🤖

A coach broke down on the highway with 50 of our guests inside. One of our vendors got in his car and drove four hours through the night to fix it. AI didn't hire that man. I did, eight years ago.

The Prague fixer who can open a restaurant's private room on two hours' notice? AI can't text him. I can.

The AI tools are genuinely useful, and we use them. But clients come back for the 8th time because of trust — knowing that when something breaks, and it always does, the right human is already on the line.

What do you think should always stay human in events? Tell us in the comments. 💬
